refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, these should be separated into distinct sub-FRs. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain representation, which is a fundamental aspect of worksite area analysis.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide accurate terrain data of the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating terrain with relevant site features and safety zones
Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational policy overlays
Produce an abstracted spatial representation supporting dynamic updates and integration with planning modules
selected_dp.selected_dp: Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational policy overlays

potential_sources: Integration of terrain data and operational policies may create dependencies if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: By modularizing terrain representation and policy overlays, the design supports independence and flexibility, reducing coupling between physical terrain data and operational constraints.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain data for model generation
Risk that operational policies may frequently change requiring updates to overlays
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the modular worksite model format
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the need for modular separation between terrain representation and policy overlays to ensure maintainability and support independent updates.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Contains the modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational policy overlays as part of the overall solution managing environment and operational context.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges data about terrain and operational status relevant to the worksite model.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational policies and task instructions to overlay on terrain data.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication with other system modules and external data sources for terrain and policy updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the worksite model is central to the NGHE Solution's environment management; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle uses the model but does not contain it; would cause high diffusion if contained here.
Teleoperation relies on the model for remote control but is not the owner; containment poor.
Safety Services may use overlays for alerts but does not own the model; moderate diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the modular worksite model because it logically centralizes terrain and policy data management, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely data updates from external sources may cause coupling through Network Link interface.
If operational policies change frequently, frequent updates may increase interface complexity.
Integration with Vehicle and Safety Services requires well-defined interfaces to avoid diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a modular worksite model that combines terrain data with operational policy overlays, directly supporting the requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Integration with Vehicle and Safety Services modules may cause diffusion if interfaces are not well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module is appropriately chosen as the IM boundary, showing high containment score (8) and low diffusion score (2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion risk. External interfaces are well identified to manage data exchange, though dependency on timely updates and frequent policy changes pose manageable risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the worksite model is contained within the NGHE Solution module, allowing direct observation and testing of the generated worksite map and policy overlays against acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is established, with well-defined interfaces to other modules, supporting traceability and ownership.

recommended_revisions: Define and document clear interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to minimize diffusion risk.
Establish update protocols and data synchronization mechanisms over the Network Link interface to manage coupling due to external data dependencies.
Monitor frequency and impact of operational policy changes to assess and mitigate interface complexity over time.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirement with a logically sound design parameter and a well-contained implementation module. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are established, and diffusion risks are identified but manageable with recommended interface and update protocols. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to consolidate the current design level and focus on implementing the recommended revisions rather than further decomposing sub-FRs at this stage.

revise.why: To define and document clear interface contracts between NGHE Solution and related modules, establish update protocols over the Network Link interface, and monitor operational policy changes to manage diffusion risks and maintain high safety standards.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module boundary is appropriate and stable for the current FR.
Interfaces to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules can be clearly defined and managed.
Operational policy changes and data synchronization can be effectively monitored and controlled.
open_risks: Potential diffusion due to integration with external modules if interfaces are not well defined.
Dependency on timely updates and frequent policy changes may increase interface complexity over time.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface contracts and update protocols.
Verification test results of the worksite model against acceptance criteria.
Monitoring reports on policy change impacts and interface performance.
